brief:
STORY ARC: discovery — Curiosity, then Discovery, then shared Joy.

Pip wakes up in the coop before Mo and Bo. The coop is dim and quiet, warm light just starting to slip through the small round window with checkered yellow curtains. She hears a strange soft sound (the dawn chorus of birds humming far away) and asks the air 'What IS that?'. She tiptoes carefully off her hay nest so she doesn't wake the others, ears tilted.

She peeks through the window. Outside, the sky is turning pink. The sun is rising over the rolling hills for the first time she's ever really noticed. Her EXTRA-LARGE eyes go wide. The light is golden. The clouds are pink. Tiny butterflies and a sleepy dragonfly start moving in the warm air.

She rushes back inside, hopping with excitement, and shakes Mo gently awake. 'Friends! Friends! There's a BIG YELLOW LIGHT coming up over the hills!' Mo blinks calmly through his round green eyeglasses and asks, slowly, 'A big yellow light?'. He tilts his head. Bo, half asleep, mishears completely — 'BANG-RISE? Are we under attack?' — and strikes a dramatic pose with one wing in the air, ready to defend the coop. Pip giggles. Bo giggles back, realizing.

Mo says, gently: 'I think... she means SUNRISE.' He explains in a calm one-liner: 'It happens every morning. That's what makes it morning.' Pip absorbs it, looking thoughtful — and then repeats it in her own words: 'So morning IS the light coming back? Every day?' Mo nods.

All three tiptoe out onto the wooden porch to watch the last of the sunrise together. The hanging lantern is still glowing softly above them. They sit close. The world looks different in this golden light — warmer, slower, almost magical. Mo says softly 'this is the best part of the day, when everything is quiet and new.' Bo whispers back 'and good for snacks.' They all laugh quietly so they don't break the calm.

Pip has her realization: 'If I'm awake first tomorrow... I'll wake YOU up.' They all giggle. The sun is fully up now, and the day begins. They head off toward the kitchen for breakfast together.
lesson: The sun rising is what makes morning, and morning is best when you share it with your friends.
mood: warm, cozy, quiet wonder building to gentle shared joy — like a picture book read aloud at bedtime, but set at dawn
angle: Pip is the audience surrogate (curious, discovers things, asks questions). Mo is the gentle explainer (one calm sentence, no lecture). Bo is the comic relief (mishears, dramatic poses, low-key snack joke). Every scene must show emotion on the characters' faces — wide-eyed wonder for discovery moments, soft warmth for shared moments.
hook: EXTREME CLOSE-UP on Pip's face in the dim coop, barely lit by pre-dawn blue light. Her EXTRA-LARGE shiny brown eyes snap open from sleep. We hear a soft humming from outside (the dawn chorus). She whispers, eyes wide with wonder: 'Oh? What IS that?'. Pull back: the coop is still dark and quiet, Mo and Bo asleep behind her on the hay. She tiptoes off her nest, ears tilted toward the sound.
